title: Leviathan (creature)
content:

{{Quote|The Leviathan didn't stop until it had devoured every last scream.|[[Kyp Durron]]|<ref name="Leviathan">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Academy: Leviathan]]''</ref>}}
The '''Leviathans''' were an ancient [[Species/Legends|species]] of bioengineered serpentine creatures [[Sith alchemy/Legends|created]] by fallen [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] during the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]]. Some sources speculated that they may have been created by [[Karness Muur]]; however, [[Sorzus Syn]] took credit for their creation in [[Book of Sith#Sorzus Syn's chronicle|her chronicle]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|That's it! You remember how! Feed upon their life energy! Once it's inside the blister traps on your back their knowledge becomes yours. And what's yours is mine!|[[Remulus Dreypa]] exhorting his Leviathans on Kesh|<ref name="LOTSS4">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 4|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:LeviathanLarva-Leviathan3.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A Leviathan larva]]
Leviathans were large, carnivorous reptiles, created to roam the battlefields as living [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapons]] and draw the life energies of enemy soldiers into [[Blister pod|blister traps]] that dotted their wide backs.<ref name="NEC" /> They were hatched from eggs, and young Leviathans appeared serpentine with no limbs, eyes, or even the species' signature blister traps. Despite this, Leviathans were aggressive towards other living organisms even at this stage of their lives. As adults, Leviathans developed into large bipedal creatures with four smaller forelimbs and two tentacles around the jaws. The hide of an adult Leviathan was incredibly tough, and enabled them to even travel through [[Magma/Legends|lava]] unscathed.<ref name="Leviathan" />

During their adult stage, Leviathans also developed several blister traps used to store the life energies of their prey.<ref name="Leviathan" /> When they killed a being, they absorbed all of the victim's life force and knowledge. Even if a being was freed from the creature's blister traps, the victim was rapidly aged and weakened.<ref name="LOTSS4" /> Adult Leviathan could also interfere with the Sense abilities of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], causing the victim to hear screams, develop headaches and trigger obsessive behavior centered around making the pain stop.<ref name="Leviathan" /> Adult Leviathans had long tentacles surrounding a gaping maw filled with rows of long teeth, two sets of eyes—one atop the other—and four nostrils in a similar arrangement. Leviathans were capable of projecting fire, which helped engender their fearsome reputation.<ref name="LOTSS4" /> While those Leviathans on [[Corbos]] had purple skin,<ref name="Leviathan" /> their offshoots on [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]] had a bluish-green hue to their skin.<ref name="LOTSS3">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 3|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 3]]</ref>

Leviathans as a species were capable of hibernating for millennia but could be awakened when prey was around.<ref name="Leviathan" /><ref name="LOTSS3" /> Leviathans could be slain through sustained [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] strikes.<ref name="LOTSS4" /> While their large size gave them an advantage over most other lifeforms in close-quarter scenarios, Leviathans could also be destroyed by concentrated fire from [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] [[Cannon/Legends|cannons]] and even [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]].<ref name="LOTSS5">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 5|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 5]]</ref> As with many other sentient and non-sentient lifeforms, Leviathans could also be electrocuted.<ref name="Leviathan" />

==Behavior and intelligence==
{{Quote|Er…They tell me you absorb knowledge with those things. Perhaps I could just read you some scrolls instead? No?|[[Grand Lord]] [[Varner Hilts]]'s first encounter with a Leviathan|<ref name="LOTSS5" />}}
[[File:JALeviathan3-FCart.jpg|thumb|right|150px|A Leviathan's blister trap]]
Despite its massive girth, the Leviathan had incredible speed and was capable of outrunning its prey. The Leviathan was also equipped with a series of blister traps on its back, which it used to drain the life energies of its prey. The beast actively hunted its prey, and when it had trapped them within its tendrils, the blister traps resonated with a bright blue aura as it began to drain the life energy and knowledge of its prey. The Leviathan hunted a diverse range of species including [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Keshiri/Legends|Keshiri]], [[S'kytri]] and even non-sapient creatures like [[uvak]]s. After digesting the life essences of their prey, the Leviathans also absorbed their knowledge and intelligence, which could be accessed by whoever controlled them. Should the beast be directly attacked by other beings, the Leviathan relied on its mass, teeth, and tendrils for self-defense.<ref name="LOTSS4" />

Despite their size and strength, the Leviathans only had the cognitive intelligence and reasoning of a [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] pet. They regarded all other beings and animals as prey to be devoured and lacked any form of intelligent speech. Instead, the Leviathans communicated through grunts and roars. The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Remulus Dreypa]] used a [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]] to direct and control the Leviathans on [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]].<ref name="LOTSS4" /> Even those not controlled by a master still posed a danger to other beings. Several Leviathans on [[Corbos]] were known to hibernate for millennia and then emerge whenever they sensed the presence of a large number of prey.<ref name="Leviathan" />

==History==
===The Hundred-Year Darkness===
{{Quote|They predate the Sith Order. The Dark Jedi that would become the first Sith Lords we know today created them during the Hundred Year Darkness, over seven thousand years ago.|[[Vul Isen]], regarding Leviathans|<ref name="Legacy32" />}}
[[File:DreypasLeviathans-Spiral4.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Leviathans devouring people]]

This species was created during the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]] and therefore predated the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. For this reason, they can be more accurately referred to as "dark-side spawn."<ref name="NEC" /> However, the term [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] has become a popular phrase during much of history.<ref name="totjc">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and future [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] priestess [[Sorzus Syn]] stated that her work creating [[Howler (Yavin 4)|Howler]], [[Shambler]], [[Pit Horror]] creatures eventual culminated in the creation of Leviathans. Her experiments with Leviathans would later help her master and improve Sith Alchemy. One specimen, called [[Krespuckle the Ever-Hungry]], became her personal favorite.<ref name="BOS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>

The [[Exiles]] created the Leviathans to reinforce their armies towards the end of the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|war]].<ref name="NEC" /> Following the [[Battle of Corbos]] in [[6900 BBY]], the Exiles left some Leviathans on [[Corbos]].<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> These Leviathans emerged whenever colonists attempted to establish themselves on the planet. In the succeeding millennia, numerous groups of colonists would be wiped out, their disappearances considered a mystery.<ref name="JA:L 2">[[Jedi Academy: Leviathan 2|''Jedi Academy: Leviathan'' 2]]</ref> Meanwhile, these Dark Jedi were exiled by the Jedi Order into the [[Stygian Caldera]],<ref name="BOS" /> where they forged the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]].<ref name="JvS" /> The Sith priestess [[Sorzus Syn]] also established a laboratory where she grew several Leviathan larvae.<ref name="BOS" />

===The Keshiri Saga===
{{Quote|Target the blister traps, my friends—and avoid close quarters! You know the tales of old. This is the fight we were born for!|[[Kaliska (S'kytri)|Kaliska]]|<ref name="LOTSS4" />}}
[[File:Leviathans_in_Tahv.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Leviathans ravage [[Tahv]] on [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]]]]
At some point following their exile, the Sith Lord and former [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Remulus Dreypa]] and some of his fellow exiles decided to leave the Sith Empire and take revenge on the Jedi Order and their weak Republic. They then assembled a strike force, gathering their new armies and several Leviathans. Those Sith Lords left their recalcitrant brothers behind them and ventured out of the Stygian Caldera. But Dreypa and his forces came across a Jedi patrol composed of Corbos veterans looking for the Exiles, to make sure that they were unable to try to seek revenge on the Jedi and Republic. After a pursuit across the galaxy, the Jedi and Sith ships crash landed on Kesh, where the space battle became a tremendous clash of lightsaber and Force-powers. During the [[Great Calamity]], several Leviathans were unleashed and caused extensive damage. Many of the [[Keshiri/Legends|Keshiri]] natives were also killed during the fighting.<ref name="LOTSS2">[[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 2|''Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral'' 2]]</ref>

However, the Sith grew disillusioned and disgusted with the damage they had wrought on the Keshiri. They had Dreypa sealed away within an [[Oubliette (stasis casket)|oubliette]] and then made peace with the Jedi. Since the two Force orders were stranded on a remote planet, they withdrew to the frozen continent of [[Eshkrene]] in the south pole where they established a new community and new order known as "[[The Doomed]]," which strove to find a balance in the Force.<ref name="LOTSS2" /> Prior to his captivity, however, Dreypa had hidden several Leviathan larvae within the volcanic crater at [[Sessal Spire]]. These Leviathan would remain dormant beneath the volcano for several millennia until [[2974 BBY]]. Following his escape, Dreypa led a peasant revolt against the ruling [[Lost Tribe of Sith]], but his forces were routed at the [[Battle of the Marisota Floodplain]]. Dreypa and his local quisling [[Parlan Spinner]] lured the Tribe's commanders to the volcano, where they unleashed the Leviathans.<ref name="LOTSS3" />

The Leviathans quickly wiped out much of the Lost Tribe's forces and then destroyed an army of "Doomed" adherents who had flown on a flock of [[uvak]]s in a desperate attempt to stop Dreypa. During the fighting, Spinner switched sides and helped save the lives of his former enemies—the [[Grand Consort]] [[Iliana Hilts]] and her daughter [[Takara Hilts|Takara]]—from being devoured by a Leviathan. He managed to wound and temporarily immobilize the Leviathan grabbing the Grand Consort and her daughter. While Spinner had managed to stop the monster before it could steal her entire life force, Iliana was transformed into an old woman.<ref name="LOTSS4" /> Dreypa's Leviathans then laid siege to the Lost Tribe's capital at Tahv, causing the city extensive damage.<ref name="LOTSS5" />

However, they were stopped by Spinner who had stolen an ancient [[Last Hope (Jedi starship)|Jedi starship]] hidden under the city and he used the ship's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projector to drag one of the Leviathans before killing it with [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannons. Spinner then proceeded to destroy most of the remaining Leviathans with the ''Last Hope''{{'s}} armaments. The last remaining Leviathan was then slain by the Sith princess Takara who nearly beheaded it with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. Dreypa himself perished at the end of the [[Siege of Tahv]] when Spinner locked the starship's autopilot and sent it on a one-way journey into [[Sessal Spire]]'s volcanic caldera. Spinner and Takara survived the battle and the Lost Tribe rebuilt its damaged capital and consolidated its power over Kesh. The former outcast himself became one of the [[Grand Lord's Hands]] which greatly improved his social standing within the tribe.<ref name="LOTSS5" />

===The New Republic===
{{Quote|Corbos…Mining Colony! We are under attack! Some sort of…creature. Destroying our entire city! We have no weapons against—Send help, please!|One of the Corbos miners as the Leviathan destroys them|<ref name="Leviathan" />}}
[[File:JALeviathan-TPB.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Members of Skywalker's [[New Jedi Order]] battling a Leviathan on Corbos]]
In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] established a [[Corbos Mining City|mining colony]] on [[Corbos]]. However, a Leviathan attacked the settlement and devoured the workers, draining their life energies. The Corbos miners managed to send a distress transmission to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. With the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|Combined Defense Forces of the New Republic]] still preoccupied with the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]], [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] convinced [[Chancellor (New Republic)|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] to ask [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to send Jedi students from the new [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to deal with the Corbos problem.<ref name="Leviathan" />

Master Skywalker dispatched the new [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyp Durron]] and one of the Academy's newest students—[[Dorsk 82]], a clone who was created to replace the deceased [[Dorsk 81]], as was his planet's custom. Upon arriving, Kyp and Dorsk found that the entire Corbos Mining City had been destroyed. As they searched through the ruins they found broken weapons and blood, showing that the miners had been killed. However, Kyp was troubled because he could hear the miners screaming for help through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], causing him to think they were still alive. Upon finding more blood he realized that they must be dead. Kyp then suggested that they sleep in the ruins and search for the creature the next day. However, Kyp was unable to sleep due to the constant screaming and wailing he sensed through the Force. He went alone to find the beast.<ref name="Leviathan" />

When Dorsk 82 woke up and found that Kyp was gone, he contacted the Jedi Academy and requested for reinforcements. Meanwhile, Kyp's trail led him to a steep cliff where he found the fossil of a deceased Leviathan. Kyp continued to sense the screams of the deceased and miners and decided that he must continue to find the source. Back on Yavin IV, Skywalker dispatched two more Jedi—[[Kirana Ti]] and [[Streen]]—to go and help Kyp and Dorsk on Corbos, having received Dorsk 82's call for help. After climbing another cliff, Kyp finally encountered the Leviathan.
After goading the beast, Kyp engaged the Leviathan in a vicious battle and inflicted numerous lightsaber wounds before finally using a grappling hook to climb onto its back. There, he discovered the ghoulish faces of the miners within a milky portal which turned out to be the creature's blister trap. Kyp used his lightsaber to penetrate the blister and free the trapped spirits.<ref name="Leviathan" />

However, the Leviathan flung the Jedi off its back and grabbed Kyp with its tendrils, intending to force him down its maw. Meanwhile Kirana and Streen quickly teamed up with Dorsk 82 to search for Kyp within the mines. They also encountered several Leviathan larvae which they proceeded to kill. Meanwhile, Kyp managed to break free of the Leviathan by unleashing [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] which fried the monster. He then released the miners' spirits from the blister traps of the deceased creature. By then, the other Jedi had succeeded in climbing out of the mines and found Kyp standing by the body of the monster. Dorsk 82 gave Kyp his lightsaber back, which he had picked up on the way out. Without warning, a much larger Leviathan appeared and attacked the four Jedi.<ref name="Leviathan" />

After a fierce battle, the Jedi team managed to force the monster into a volcanic crater and, believing it to be dead, stood around the lava. However, Kyp commented that he could still hear the terrible screaming in his head and his words were confirmed as the monster emerged unscathed from the lava. The Jedi continued to fight it, trying to avoid the drops of lava now falling all around them. Eventually Dorsk 82 discovered a functioning power generator and convinced Kyp to attach the cables around the monster in order to electrocute it. Kyp succeeded in entangling the Leviathan within the cables and Dorsk 82 hit the switch. Instantly, the huge monster was engulfed with electricity and was killed.<ref name="Leviathan" />

Following Kyp's mission, several archaeological teams visited the planet in the following years, but they didn't report any Leviathan sightings, supporting the conclusion that the species was extinct. Regardless, colonists and miners continued to avoid the planet under the belief that some of the creatures may have survived underground, even though there was no proof of this.<ref name="JvS" />
[[File:LeviathanKills.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sea Leviathan, its aquatic cousin]]

===The Legacy era===
{{Main|Sea Leviathan}}
In [[137 ABY]], [[One Sith]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Vul Isen]] of the [[One Sith]] engineered a species of [[Sea Leviathan]] to aid in the [[Genocide on Dac|genocide]] on the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. Following the destruction of the beast, [[Master]] [[Treis Sinde]] reported to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] that the One Sith may recreate and mass produce the land based species as the conflict in the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] raged on.<ref name="SWLFAD">''[[Star Wars: Legacy: Fight Another Day]]''</ref> However, the failure of the Sea Leviathan and the growing threat of the resurgent Jedi and the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] ended further Sith plans to spawn more Leviathans for the war effort.<ref name="leg47">[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]]</ref>

Unwilling to commit more resources to Dac, the [[Regent/Legends|Sith Regent]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] instead ordered Vul Isen to implement the [[Final Protocol]]: the destruction of all life on the planet. Satisfied with the prospect of continuing his work elsewhere, Isen infected Dac's oceans with [[viral spore]]s, which would be carried by oceanic currents and kill every life form on the planet within a week. Three days later, Dac's oceans were already covered with the bodies of the dead. Isen's action caused the deaths of more than eighty percent of the planet's population, and would have caused total extermination if not for the intervention of the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]] and an evacuation fleet of civilian volunteers.<ref name="leg47" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Leviathans first appeared as antagonists in [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[Star Wars: Jedi Academy: Leviathan|''Jedi Academy: Leviathan'']] comic series, which ran from [[October]] [[1998]] to [[January]] [[1999]]. In this comic series, the Leviathans were drawn by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]]; inked by [[Mark G. Heike]], [[Bill Black]], and [[David Jacob Beckett]]; and colored by [[Ray Murtaugh]]. [[Daniel Wallace]] and Anderson's ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which was released on [[October 25]], [[2005]], revealed the Leviathans to be [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] created by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who instigated the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]]. Wallace's ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'', which was released on [[February 10]], [[2012]], identified the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles|Exile]] [[Sorzus Syn]] as the creator of the Leviathans.

Leviathans also made a cameo flashback appearance in [[John Ostrander]] and [[Omar Francia]]'s ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' series when they appeared in [[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]], which debuted on [[November 26]], [[2008]]. Leviathans later provided a conceptual basis for the aquatic [[Sea Leviathan]], which debuted in the ninth ''Legacy'' story arc, [[Star Wars: Legacy: Fight Another Day|''Fight Another Day'']]. This story arc focused on developments in the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] and consisted of two issues, running from [[January 28]] to [[February 29]], [[2009]]. Leviathans also feature as minions of the antagonistic [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Remulus Dreypa in [[John Jackson Miller]]'s standalone [[Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral|''Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith'']] comic series, which ran from [[August 8]] to [[December 12]], [[2012]]. They debuted at the cliffhanger of [[Lost Tribe of the Sith — Spiral 3|''Lost Tribe of the Sith—Spiral 3'']], which was first released on [[October 10]], [[2012]]. For this standalone comic, the Leviathans were drawn by [[Andrea Mutti]], inked by [[Pierluigi Baldassini]], and colored by [[Michael Atiyeh]].
